steps.title: Identify the goal
steps.content: The student wants to refute the claim that slow TV is boring using a quotation. The answer must (1) push back on the boring claim, (2) include a quote that supports the refutation.

steps.title: Match the choice
steps.content:
(C) opens with "Far from boring" — explicit refutation — and quotes Heller's praise ("visceral kind of armchair tourism") as the supporting evidence.

(A) doesn't refute the boring claim. (B) just presents both views without taking a side. (D) endorses the boring claim instead of refuting it.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
